concern_reasons.note: 11 clippy warnings visible in workspace runs are from other crates, not mqo-concept-graph. Crate-level `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` exits 0 cleanly.
All tests are in tests/ integration files. Internal helpers (str_field, parse_edge_kind) are tested indirectly. Acceptable for a pure-library crate with no private state.

falsification.test_audit: AC1: ac1_node_count_matches_fixture counts exactly 15 nodes (3 measures + 3 hierarchies + 7 levels + 2 calcs); test fails if any type is miscounted. ac1_all_unique_names_are_accessible verifies every unique_name is indexable. AC2: ac2_edges_are_typed_enum_variants uses exhaustive match that would fail to compile if EdgeKind became a String — compile-time type guarantee. ac2_all_four_primary_edge_kinds_present verifies all 4 primary edge kinds appear in a fixture that explicitly contains them. AC3: 6 independent error cases tested (empty string, whitespace, invalid JSON, JSON array, truncated JSON, null). AC4: 6 tests covering all EdgeKind variants for filtering, empty iterator for wrong kind, empty iterator for unknown node, and hierarchy-to-level containment via RelatedTo. All 20 tests are falsifiable.
falsification.panic_audit: Reviewed src/lib.rs: zero unwrap() or expect() calls in production paths. All fallible paths return Result<_, ConceptGraphError> and propagate via ?. No indexing into slices (iterator-only). No panic! macros. str_field and parse_edge_kind return Err variants. Verdict: panic-safe.
falsification.unsafe_audit: #![deny(unsafe_code)] at crate root. No unsafe{} blocks. petgraph 0.6.5, serde, serde_json are safe-Rust crates. Verdict: no unsafe code.
falsification.public_api_audit: All pub types have doc comments enforced by #![deny(missing_docs)]: ConceptGraphError, ConceptNode, NodeKind, ConceptEdge, EdgeKind, EdgeSnapshot, ConceptGraphSnapshot, ConceptGraph. All pub methods documented. ConceptGraph uses snapshot pattern (to_snapshot/from_snapshot) for serialization — deliberate choice to avoid petgraph internal format coupling.
falsification.deps_audit: 3 prod dependencies: serde@1, serde_json@1, petgraph@0.6.5. All MIT/Apache-2 licensed. No network, DB, or LLM deps. max_deps=8 constraint satisfied. Verdict: clean.
falsification.drift_audit: Intent-card scope: 4 NodeKind variants (Measure, DimensionLevel, Calc, Hierarchy), 5 EdgeKind variants (aggregates_via, time_shifts, filters_by, derives_from, related_to), traversal API, structured errors, no external I/O. Implementation provides exactly these. Non-goals (no persistence, no LLM calls, no CLI, no mutation) all satisfied. Verdict: no drift.

counter_attack.description: Attack 1 on AC1: dimension object without 'hierarchies' key — code uses .and_then().unwrap_or(&[]) so silently produces 0 hierarchies/levels for that dimension. The AC1 test fixture always has hierarchies, so the test still passes. Attack 2 on AC3: pass the JSON string value '"hello"' (valid JSON, not object) — root.is_object() returns false, correctly returns Err(InvalidStructure). Attack 3 on AC4: add two edges of the same kind between same nodes — neighbors() returns both (duplicates preserved). This is a known property of DiGraph and is acceptable behavior for v1.
